tasks.title: Auto-detection only runs on every 5th email (contacts every 10th), silently dropping ~80% of results
tasks.description: HomePage.tsx:232-243 gates detection on `if (result.emails % 5 === 0)` and contact tracking on `% 10`, where `result.emails` is the running import counter. Whether an email is scanned for accounts/purchases/subscriptions/newsletters depends on its arbitrary position in the import stream, not its content — so ~80% of detectable accounts/purchases/subscriptions/newsletters and ~90% of contacts are never recorded, leaving the app's core Auto-Discovery pages mostly empty. Detection (runDetection/trackContact) is just regex over already-parsed strings, so the 'save time on large imports' rationale doesn't hold. Run detection and contact tracking on every email (optionally yielding to the event loop in batches).

tasks.title: Detected purchase currency is discarded — every purchase stored as USD
tasks.description: purchaseDetector.detectPurchase computes the real currency (EUR/GBP/JPY/CAD/AUD/INR/CHF) and returns it in DetectionResult.data.currency (purchaseDetector.ts:314-346), but createPurchaseFromEmail (purchaseDetector.ts:488-499) hardcodes `currency: 'USD'` and takes no currency argument, and HomePage.tsx:111-118 never passes it through. A €50,00 order is persisted as $50.00 USD, so PurchasesPage and SpendingChart mislabel and mis-aggregate every non-USD purchase. Thread the detected currency into createPurchaseFromEmail and the insertPurchase call.

tasks.title: Newsletter detector domain prefix check false-positives on gmail.com/hotmail.com
tasks.description: newsletterDetector.ts:66-78 stores bare prefixes ('mail.', 'news.', 'email.') in knownPromotionalDomains and matches them with `domain.includes(prefix)` (line 129). 'gmail.com' and 'hotmail.com' both contain the substring 'mail.', so every email from a Gmail/Hotmail sender gets a +20 newsletter/promotional score; combined with a common unsubscribe link (+10/15) this misclassifies ordinary personal mail as promotional. Match against a subdomain boundary (e.g. domain.split('.')[0] === prefix or domain.startsWith(prefix)) instead of a naive substring includes.

tasks.title: Import inserts emails one-per-transaction instead of using bulkInsertEmails
tasks.description: processEmailBatch (HomePage.tsx:216-248) calls insertEmail(email) in a loop, and insertEmail (database.ts:176-201) opens its own db.transaction('rw', ...) per row. The worker already delivers emails in batches of 100, but each batch becomes 100 separate IndexedDB transactions; a large archive (tens of thousands of emails) pays a per-row transaction cost it doesn't need. database.ts already exports bulkInsertEmails (database.ts:250-271) which writes a whole batch in one transaction and returns the ids. Use it per worker batch, then run detection over the returned ids.

tasks.title: Subscription dedup by sender domain never matches (dead lookup branch)
tasks.description: HomePage.tsx:128-131 tries getSubscriptionByServiceName(senderDomain) as a fallback to dedupe subscriptions, but subscriptions are only ever stored keyed by their human serviceName (e.g. 'Netflix', 'Microsoft 365'), never by raw domain (insertSubscription / detectSubscription set serviceName to the friendly name). The domain lookup therefore always returns undefined, so the same service detected under slightly different names creates duplicate subscription rows. Either store/normalize a domain key on subscriptions or dedupe on the resolved domain explicitly.

tasks.title: Threading docstring promises participant-overlap matching that is not implemented
tasks.description: threadingService.ts:11 and the class comment claim threads are matched by '3) participant overlap', but getThreadKey (threadingService.ts:48-71) only uses threadId or a domain-scoped normalized-subject key. Generic subjects ('Invoice', 'Receipt', 'Your order') from the SAME sender domain still collapse into one conversation, and genuine reply chains that lost their threadId headers but share participants are not rejoined. Either implement the participant/sender-overlap pass described, or update the docstring to match the two-step reality.

tasks.title: Email preview snippets render a stray/double ellipsis
tasks.description: makeSnippet (mimeUtils.ts:121) already appends '…' when it truncates, but EmailCard.tsx:144 and ThreadView.tsx:78,176,264 render `{preview}{preview ? '...' : ''}`, unconditionally appending '...'. Truncated snippets show '…...' and short, non-truncated snippets get a misleading trailing '...'. Drop the manual '...' suffix and rely on the snippet's own ellipsis (or only append when actually truncated).

tasks.title: Remove dead exports left over from the lazy-body refactor
tasks.description: prefetchEmailBody (hooks/useLazyEmailBody.ts:73-79) is exported but has no importers, and EmailCard.tsx:9 / ThreadView.tsx:9 still carry the duplicated `deriveSnippet` helper behind a stale `TODO(parsing-bucket): replace with makeSnippet ... once it lands` comment even though makeSnippet has shipped in mimeUtils.ts. Delete the unused prefetch helper and replace the two deriveSnippet copies with the shared makeSnippet to remove the dead TODO and duplication.

summary: Found 8 issues: 1 high (auto-detection samples only every 5th email / contacts every 10th, silently dropping ~80% of accounts/purchases/subscriptions/newsletters and ~90% of contacts), 4 medium (purchase currency discarded and stored as USD, newsletter domain-prefix substring false-positives on gmail/hotmail, one-transaction-per-email import instead of bulkInsertEmails, dead subscription-by-domain dedup lookup), and 3 low (unimplemented participant-overlap threading vs docstring, double/stray ellipsis in previews, dead prefetch + duplicated deriveSnippet). Categories: 1 data-completeness bug, 2 data-quality bugs, 1 performance, 1 dead-branch logic, 1 correctness/docs mismatch, 2 cleanup.
_note: This file was regenerated against the CURRENT source; the prior version was largely stale. Re-verified as ALREADY FIXED and excluded: searchParser is now wired into EmailsPage.tsx:129-131 via parseSearchQuery+filterEmails; the live worker enforces MAX_COMPRESSED_BYTES/MAX_DECOMPRESSED_BYTES and MAX_SUBJECT/BODY/EMAIL caps (parserWorker.ts:790-807,447-456); isMboxFromLine uses a strict envelope regex and ignores '>From' (mimeUtils.ts:97-101); quoted-printable/RFC2047 decode is charset-aware with surrogate handling (mimeUtils.ts:12-89); isDomainMatch is boundary-safe (domainMatch.ts) and used by all three detectors; subscription amount/frequency are billing-context-anchored (subscriptionDetector.ts:197-241); custom-rule regex input is length-bounded (customRulesEngine.ts:157-163); backup import is fully transactional with version + NaN-date validation (backupService.ts:288-451); the store uses applyInPlace for O(1) single-row updates and surfaces failures via toast (store/index.ts:46-57); store init loads header rows only via getEmailHeaders; base64 conversion is chunked (utils/base64.ts); generatePassphrase uses the bundled wordlist with rejection sampling (encryptionService.ts:283-310); keyboard 'g' sequence tracks a single ref-held handler/timeout (useKeyboardShortcuts.ts); HTML email bodies are sanitized with DOMPurify (EmailDetailPage.tsx:305-321); threads are built from the folder-filtered set via buildThreadsForView (EmailsPage.tsx:155-174); parseAmount now handles locale comma/dot decimal conventions (purchaseDetector.ts:349-398).
